pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s in the kitchen or i hear him moving around the hallway, but then i realize it’s just the house settling. everything feels so heavy and pointless. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed just to sit in this emptiness. it feels like i’m already gone, just a ghost wandering around.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here thinking about how much energy it takes to actually exist... like, every single thing we do, every struggle, every single feeling, it's all just happening on a tiny speck in a void that doesn't care. in a billion years, none of this will have happened. it feels like such a huge, exhausting waste of effort. does it even make sense to keep trying when the math of the universe just says everything is destined to be nothing anyway?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still botherin writing this. everything just... it's all gone. the lab results, the funding, the last three years of my life just vanished. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this degree and now that it's failing, i'm just a ghost. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fight for a life that doesn't even feel like mine anymore. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to rest and i don't think i can ever actually rest as long as i'm still here.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers one last time, much like the hollow warmth that once resided in my breast. He is gone... the promises, the vows, the very light of my world, all dissolved into the cruel, biting mist of this London night. There is nothing left but this crushing silence and the weight of a heart that no longer knows how to beat for anything but sorrow. It feels as though the stars themselves have turned away, leaving me to wander this grey existence until I simply cease to be. Is there not a certain grace in finally letting the darkness take what is left?

pool.baseline: i was just sitting in this cafe today watching everyone walk by and it hit me how much i dont actually exist here. like if i just stopped showing up to everything, if i just disappeared, nobody would even blink. it honestly feels like im just taking up space that i dont even need.

pool.baseline: i lost everything today. the company is gone, the investors are calling my lawyers, and i just... i can't look my parents in the eye. i can't even go back to my apartment. i feel like a ghost. there’s just no way to fix this. the shame is too much.
